evidenceChecked: content/080-transfer.md#Transfer task and Model response after your attempt
content/090-assessment.md#Answer and feedback conditions through Remediation routes
AUTHORING-BRIEF.md#Target and learner through Feedback, transfer, retention, and remediation

findings.finding: The item-2 remediation tells the learner to return to “the planning table,” but both the concept scene and the worked-example scene contain planning tables. The ambiguous locator can add avoidable navigation and search load at the moment the learner is trying to repair a weak plan.
findings.resolution: Name or link the intended scene and table, for example “the six-part planning table in the Concept scene.”

findings.finding: The mapped graph outcome is carried through three observable objectives into a stable six-part explanatory model, modelled use, varied generation, interpretation of attempt evidence, and scored plan construction and adaptation. The assessment requires constructed reasoning and changed-context use in addition to the single recognition item, so the provisional mastery gate is not based on familiarity or one fluent encounter.

The complete worked plan exposes constraints, model selection, decisions, checks, and interpretation; support then fades through completion, supported choice, independent design, mixed discrimination, and a group-coordination transfer task whose constraints differ materially from the modelled individual case.

Retrieval prompts precede answer reveal, feedback identifies the governing relation and next action, and the misconception sequence elicits a prediction, contrasts the plausible and replacement models on the same case, and rechecks the distinction in a new case.

limitations: No direct observations, interviews, delayed-performance data, or other empirical evidence from actual foundation-level Premed learners were available, so comprehension, timing, and retention effects remain untested.
This role evaluated learning design and assessment alignment; it did not independently decide academic-source accuracy or source-reuse rights.
The review used the canonical Markdown and JSON sources and deterministic validation environment; it did not exercise the compiled graphical reader or an interactive learner delivery session.
